title: Projects
text:
A selection of quantitative finance and software projects completed during my studies at the University of Alberta.



QTP · Fin-452


WTI Options Portfolio: Full Greek P&L Attribution


Simulation of an energy trading desk risk role. Constructs a complete Greek P&L attribution framework — Delta, Gamma, Vega, Vanna, and Theta — for a portfolio of WTI crude oil options on NYMEX CL futures. Prices options using Black-Scholes and models realistic scenario shifts across underlying price, implied volatility, and time decay.


R Black-Scholes Options Pricing Risk Attribution


View Report →




QTP · Fin-452


Momentum-Based Sector Rotation Strategy


Dollar-neutral long-short strategy across the 11 SPDR S&P 500 sector ETFs. Applies Kalman filtering to smooth rolling momentum, volatility, and Sharpe ratio features, then uses hierarchical clustering (Ward’s D2) to generate monthly rebalance signals. Evaluated over a 24-year training window and out-of-sample period with a full parameter grid search.


R Kalman Filter Hierarchical Clustering Backtesting Momentum


View Report →




Options Analysis · Fin-452


Options Greeks Analysis


Foundational analysis of options pricing sensitivities through the Greeks framework. Examines Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega, and Rho across varying strike, maturity, and volatility conditions for WTI crude oil options. Includes Greek surface visualizations and explores their practical use in hedging and position risk management.


R Options Greeks Black-Scholes Hedging


View Report →




Shiny App · Fin-451


Energy Risk Dashboard


Production-grade Shiny dashboard built with the Golem framework for energy commodity risk managers. Covers WTI (Cushing & Houston), Brent, Heating Oil, RBOB, and Natural Gas futures. Modules include forward curve analysis, rolling minimum-variance hedge ratios with basis risk decomposition, GARCH volatility regimes, EIA petroleum fundamentals, and U.S. Treasury yield curves. EIA and FRED data refresh nightly via GitHub Actions.


R Shiny Golem Docker GitHub Actions EIA API


View on GitHub →




Valuation · NPV@Risk


Upgrader Project Valuation — Suncor


Full valuation of a $2B Cold Lake Bitumen-to-Synthetic Crude upgrader investment for Suncor using NPV@Risk methodology. Models the SYN-CL upgrading spread with an Ornstein-Uhlenbeck with Jumps (OUJ) stochastic process, simulating 20 years of monthly cash flows across thousands of scenarios. Stress tests include geopolitical shocks, government-backed hydrocracking, and escalating carbon taxation.


R Monte Carlo Ornstein-Uhlenbeck DCF Energy Finance


View Report →

title: Alex Hirtle - Portfolio
text:
Alex Hirtle, Ualberta Finance Honours
Alex Hirtle is a fourth year Finance Honours student studying at the University of Alberta School of Business.
Before enrolling in University, Alex started a small business importing cars from Japan. Through his experiences building a customer base, dealing with the export, import, customs, and accounting process he honed his skills in CRM, logistics, budgeting, marketing, and sales. Throughout his studies, Alex continues to import a number of vehicles, doing most of the work from the seat of his motorcycle while travelling across B.C. and Alberta during his summer break.
Additionally, Alex has worked on academic research papers with professors from UCLA, U of A, SFU, and Cambridge. He provided quantitative reports, data cleaning and procurement, and industry reports for researchers to further use in their publishing.
Alex has become well known among other finance students and practitioners around the university as a hardworking, reliable, out of the box thinker who isn’t afraid to learn something new.
